questions.question: Why are statistics important in machine learning?
options: A) They help interpret data, uncertainty, distributions, and evaluation results
B) They only matter for frontend analytics
D) They are optional in all ML work
questions.correctAnswer: A) They help interpret data, uncertainty, distributions, and evaluation results

questions.question: What is the role of linear algebra in ML?
options: A) It underpins vectors, matrices, transformations, and many model computations
B) It only matters for CSS grids
D) It is only for finance
questions.correctAnswer: A) It underpins vectors, matrices, transformations, and many model computations

questions.question: Why is Python commonly used in ML workflows?
options: A) Because of its ecosystem for data analysis, modeling, and experimentation
B) Because compiled languages cannot do ML
C) Because Python removes the need for math
D) Because browsers train models faster
questions.correctAnswer: A) Because of its ecosystem for data analysis, modeling, and experimentation

questions.question: What does data cleaning primarily address?
options: A) Missing values, inconsistencies, outliers, and quality issues before modeling
questions.correctAnswer: A) Missing values, inconsistencies, outliers, and quality issues before modeling

questions.question: Why is train-test separation important?
options: A) It helps estimate how a model may behave on unseen data
questions.correctAnswer: A) It helps estimate how a model may behave on unseen data

questions.question: What is feature engineering about?
options: A) Creating or transforming inputs to improve model usefulness
questions.correctAnswer: A) Creating or transforming inputs to improve model usefulness

questions.question: Why is data leakage dangerous?
options: A) It can make a model look stronger during evaluation than it really is in production
D) It only happens in unsupervised learning
questions.correctAnswer: A) It can make a model look stronger during evaluation than it really is in production

questions.question: What is the difference between regression and classification?
options: A) Regression predicts continuous values, classification predicts categories
B) Regression is unsupervised and classification is always deep learning
questions.correctAnswer: A) Regression predicts continuous values, classification predicts categories

questions.question: Why are evaluation metrics chosen carefully?
options: A) Different problems need different measures of success and tradeoffs
B) Metrics only matter after deployment
questions.correctAnswer: A) Different problems need different measures of success and tradeoffs

questions.question: What is overfitting?
options: A) When a model learns training patterns too specifically and performs poorly on new data
B) When a model is too small to train
questions.correctAnswer: A) When a model learns training patterns too specifically and performs poorly on new data

questions.question: Why is cross-validation useful?
options: A) It provides a stronger estimate of model stability across different data splits
C) It only matters for neural networks
questions.correctAnswer: A) It provides a stronger estimate of model stability across different data splits

questions.question: What is clustering used for?
options: A) Grouping similar data points without explicit labels
questions.correctAnswer: A) Grouping similar data points without explicit labels

questions.question: Why are neural networks useful?
options: A) They can model complex patterns when simpler methods are insufficient
B) They remove the need for data quality
C) They always outperform classical models
D) They only work on images
questions.correctAnswer: A) They can model complex patterns when simpler methods are insufficient

questions.question: What is the purpose of backpropagation?
options: A) Updating model parameters based on prediction error through the network
questions.correctAnswer: A) Updating model parameters based on prediction error through the network

questions.question: Why is experiment tracking important?
options: A) It helps compare runs, hyperparameters, and reproducibility over time
B) It only matters for notebooks
questions.correctAnswer: A) It helps compare runs, hyperparameters, and reproducibility over time

questions.question: What is model drift?
options: A) Performance degradation caused by changes in data or real-world conditions over time
questions.correctAnswer: A) Performance degradation caused by changes in data or real-world conditions over time

questions.question: Why is model deployment a separate concern from model training?
options: A) Serving, latency, monitoring, and integration constraints differ from offline experimentation
B) Because deployed models do not need data
C) Because training and deployment are identical
D) Because APIs cannot serve models
questions.correctAnswer: A) Serving, latency, monitoring, and integration constraints differ from offline experimentation

questions.question: Why should ML practitioners think about bias and ethics?
options: A) Because models can amplify harmful patterns and affect real users or decisions
B) Because ethics only matters in law
C) Because bias improves every model
questions.correctAnswer: A) Because models can amplify harmful patterns and affect real users or decisions

questions.question: What is the value of a baseline model?
options: A) It gives a simple reference point before more complex approaches are adopted
C) It is always the best model
questions.correctAnswer: A) It gives a simple reference point before more complex approaches are adopted

questions.question: What is the strongest way to learn machine learning effectively?
options: A) Build end-to-end projects that include data prep, modeling, evaluation, and deployment thinking
D) Focus only on notebooks without reproducibility
questions.correctAnswer: A) Build end-to-end projects that include data prep, modeling, evaluation, and deployment thinking

questions.question: Which ML algorithm is supervised?
questions.correctAnswer: B) Linear Regression

questions.question: Which ML model is used for classification?
questions.correctAnswer: B) Logistic Regression
